item.title: Iran fires missile at Kuwait after second round of U.S. strikes in days
item.description: Iran says it targeted a U.S. base in retaliation for the second set of what the Pentagon calls "purely defensive" strikes carried out this week.

item.title: Extended interview: Judd Winick reflects on MTV's "The Real World"
item.description: Judd Winick talks to CBS Mornings' Vladimir Duthiers about appearing on MTV's "The Real World: San Francisco" in 1994, the audition process for the reality TV series and more.

item.title: Extended interview: "Real Housewives" star Dorinda Medley
item.description: Dorina Medley, who appeared in the "Real Housewives of New York City" for six seasons, talks to CBS Mornings' Vladimir Duthiers about her decision to join the Bravo series, why she has no regrets and what draws people to reality TV.

item.title: American climber breaks Everest speed record, his team says
item.description: Tyler Andrews, 36, climbed the 29,032-foot peak in just 9 hours and 55 minutes, smashing the record of 10 hours and 56 minutes, his team said.

item.title: Stocks are rallying despite the Iran war and stubborn inflation. Here's why.
item.description: The S&P 500 hit a new record on Wednesday, even as soaring gas prices fuel inflation and consumer confidence sinks.

item.title: Legendary mafia leader's "vast fortune" seized by police
item.description: Italy said it was seizing $232 million in assets belonging to the late Mafia boss Matteo Messina Denaro, who was captured after 30 years on the run.

item.title: "48 Hours" show schedule
item.description: True crime. Real justice. To miss it would be a crime.

item.title: 3M sued for $1.4 billion by Australia over "forever chemicals" at defense bases
item.description: Australia is suing 3M for more than 2 billion Australian dollars ($1.4 billion) over so-called "forever chemical" contamination from firefighting foam at defense bases, the government says.

item.title: Fire at girls' school in Kenya kills at least 16 students, injures scores more
item.description: A fire at a school for girls in central Kenya has caused an unknown number of deaths, according to police, who said search teams have been deployed.

item.title: Another alleged drug boat struck by U.S. military in eastern Pacific, killing 2
item.description: The U.S. military said it struck another vessel suspected of transporting drugs in the eastern Pacific Ocean, killing two men, the day after it said a similar strike killed one man and left two survivors.

item.title: Iran "negotiating on fumes," Trump says, as U.S. conducts new strikes
item.description: The U.S. and Iran had appeared ready to de-escalate before the U.S. military conducted what it said were defensive strikes against Iran on Wednesday.

item.title: DOJ investigating whether Trump accuser E. Jean Carroll committed perjury
item.description: The Justice Department is conducting a criminal investigation into whether author E. Jean Carroll committed perjury in connection with her civil lawsuits against President Trump, sources familiar with the matter said.

item.title: 5 of 7 people trapped in flooded Laos cave for more than a week found alive
item.description: Five villagers stuck in a flooded cave in central Laos for more than a week were found alive, rescuers said Wednesday, but two others are missing.

item.title: Google employee accused of making over $1.2M by insider trading on Polymarket
item.description: A software engineer at Google is facing federal charges after allegedly betting on confidential company information on Polymarket, netting more than $1.2 million in profits.

item.title: 5/27: CBS Evening News
item.description: Latest details on Washington state chemical tank rupture; Trump's optimism on an Iran peace deal seems to waver.

item.title: Minneapolis Police Chief Brian O'Hara resigns after internal probe
item.description: Mayor Jacob Frey announced O'Hara's resignation late Tuesday after an investigation found he deleted a contact from his city-issued phone to hide his connection to an employee during a prior investigation.

item.title: 5/27: The Takeout with Major Garrett
item.description: Takeaways from Texas primary runoffs; Trump paints murky picture of Iran peace talks.

item.title: Ex-CIA official arrested after $40M in gold bars allegedly found at his home
item.description: A former CIA official was arrested after FBI agents allegedly found $40 million worth of gold bars at his home while investigating whether he lied about his background.

item.title: CEOs for S&P 500 companies had 6% pay increase in 2025, AP survey finds
item.description: New data shows the median compensation for CEOs of S&P 500 companies grew almost 6% in 2025, reaching $17.7 million. This uptick marks the smallest annual growth rate since 2022, when the median pay grew just about 1%. The figures come from the Associated Press' CEO compensation survey. Ken Sweet, a banking reporter for the AP who helped write the analysis, joins "The Daily Report" to discuss.

item.title: Teen accused of killing stepsister on Carnival Cruise to remain free for now
item.description: Timothy Hudson, the 16-year-old charged with sexually assaulting and killing his 18-year-old stepsister, Anna Kepner, will remain free for now while a judge considers arguments following a hearing Wednesday. CBS News' Katrina Kaufman has the latest updates.

item.title: 2 dead, 9 workers "unrecovered" after Washington chemical tank implosion
item.description: The damaged tank at Nippon Dynawave Packaging Co. held approximately 900,000 gallons of white liquor, a chemical used in paper processing, authorities said.

item.title: Uganda closes its border with Congo over Ebola outbreak
item.description: Uganda on Wednesday ordered the closure of its border with Congo, where suspected cases of a rare type of Ebola are surging.

item.title: U.S. carries out new strikes against Iranian military site, official says
item.description: The U.S. military carried out another round of strikes on Iran, a U.S. official confirmed to CBS News on Wednesday, another challenge to a shaky ceasefire between the two countries.

item.title: Doctor breaks down peptide safety
item.description: Some health officials telling medical professionals to stop prescribing certain peptides. CBS News medical correspondent Dr. Céline Gounder breaks down what exactly peptides are and why some doctors are concerned.

item.title: Ex-judges push to look into "anti-weaponization fund" deal
item.description: A group of 35 former federal judges asked a court to reopen a legal dispute between President Trump and the government, calling the deal to create a $1.776 billion "anti-weaponization fund" potentially fraudulent.

item.title: Trump visited soldiers at Walter Reed — but not the 14 injured in the Iran war
item.description: More than a dozen soldiers injured in Operation Epic Fury are still recovering at the military hospital.

item.title: Takeaways from Texas' primaries: Trump's power, Latino vote, redistricting and more
item.description: The November ballot in Texas is now set, after a four-term Republican senator lost to a Trump-endorsed challenger in Tuesday's primary runoffs. Meanwhile, Texas Democrats are angling to win their first Senate race since 1988.

item.title: Justice Dept. to start fast-tracking benefits fraud enforcement
item.description: The U.S. Department of Justice tells CBS News it will speed up review of certain whistleblower complaints dealing with fraud against benefits programs like Medicare.

item.title: Jill Biden: "I was frightened" by 2024 debate, thought husband was having a stroke
item.description: Former first lady Jill Biden said she thought her husband, Joe Biden, was having a stroke during the 2024 debate against Donald Trump.

item.title: How much does a new HVAC system really cost in 2026?
item.description: HVAC costs have been surging recently, but some homeowners are paying far more than necessary for their systems.
